revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24598347618|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:安達太良神社]] {{Expand Japanese|安達太良神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Adatara Shrine | native_name = 安達太良神社 | image = 安達太良神社 - panoramio.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Adatara Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.521156|140.40435}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Takamimusubi|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|de|Takamimusubi|en|Takamimusubi|es|Takami-musubi-no-kami|fa|تاکامیموسوبی|fr|Takamimusubi|id|Takamimusubi|ja|タカミムスヒ|ks|تاکامِمُسُبی|pl|Takamimusubi|ru|Такамимусуби-но ками|zh|高皇產靈尊|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} }} {{nihongo|'''Adatara Shrine'''|安達太良神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 92 ** P155: {{ill|Q17217367|ltq=Q17217367|qid=Q17217367|1=Unakorowake Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Okitsushimano Shrine|lt=Okitsushimano Shrine|qid=Q135040047|1=Okitsushimano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q867106|ltq=Q867106|qid=Q867106|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}} * {{ill|Mutsu Province|lt=Mutsu Province|ca|Província de Mutsu|cs|Provincie Mucu|de|Provinz Mutsu|en|Mutsu Province|es|Provincia de Mutsu|fa|ولایت موتسو|fi|Mutsun provinssi|fr|Province de Mutsu|gl|Provincia de Mutsu|id|Provinsi Mutsu|it|Mutsu (provincia)|ja|陸奥国|ko|무쓰국|lt|Mucu žemė|nl|Mutsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Mutsu|ru|Муцу (провинция)|simple|Mutsu Province|sk|Mucu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Муцу|tr|Mutsu (vilayet)|uk|Муцу (провінція)|zh_classical|陸奧國|zh_min_nan|Mutu-kok|zh_yue|陸奧國|zh|陸奧國|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} * {{ill|Q11451165|ltq=Q11451165|qid=Q11451165|1=Asaka district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.521156|140.40435}} * {{coord|37.52124|140.404167}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * 安達太良神社 - panoramio.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q125400|ltq=Q125400|qid=Q125400|1=Takamimusubi}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182129 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q90139226|ltq=Q90139226|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * イヒトヨ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1380005004362 == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Ihitoyo- Shrine|lt=Ihitoyo- Shrine|qid=Q135040046|1=Ihitoyo- Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県本宮市本宮字舘越232', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '福島県郡山市三穂田町下守屋', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:安達太良神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11451521}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Adatara Shrine |Image = [[File:安達太良神社 - panoramio.jpg|250px]]
Haiden |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Motomiya City|Q867106|qid=Q867106|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}} Motomiya Aza Tatekoshi 232 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Kamimusubi}}
Iitoyowa-ke-no-Kami
Iitsuhime-no-Kami
Haruhi Onsen-no-Kami
Negi Otoji-no-Kami |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) candidate shrine
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Kyūan|Q1193860|qid=Q1193860|1=Kyūan}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1146|Q19627|qid=Q19627|1=1146}}) |Annual festivals = {{ill|lt=May 1|Q2544|qid=Q2544|1=May 1}} (Spring Festival), three days from the fourth Friday of October (Autumn Festival) |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Adatara
Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Adatara Shrine''' (あだたらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Motomiya City|Q867106|qid=Q867106|1=Motomiya, Fukushima}}, {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine of Iitoyowa-ke Shrine, a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=listed minor shrine|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a Prefectural Shrine. === Overview === The shrine is said to have been founded in {{ill|lt=1146|Q19627|qid=Q19627|1=1146}} (Kyūan 2) during the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}. It is believed to have begun when the deities of {{ill|lt=Mount Adatara|Q352675|qid=Q352675|1=Mount Adatara}} and Mount Ōnagura were enshrined and established as a satomiya (village shrine). Locals affectionately call it “Myōjin-sama.” As it is regarded as the head shrine of the Adatara Shrines scattered throughout the surrounding area, it is said that the name of the place of enshrinement changed from “Motome Village” to Motomiya Village, becoming the origin of the place name “Motomiya.” It was also once the chief guardian shrine of the former Adachi District; however, in {{ill|lt=1683|Q7651|qid=Q7651|lt=Tenna 3|1=1683}}, Suzuki Aki-no-Kami, a priest of {{ill|lt=Nihonmatsu Shrine|Q22119901|qid=Q22119901|lt=formerly Nihonmatsu Kumano Shrine|1=Nihonmatsu Shrine}} (the present Nihonmatsu Shrine), argued that “the enshrined deity of Adatara Shrine is the spirit of a person named Adachi Tarō, not the deities of Mount Adatara, and therefore it is not suitable to be the district’s chief guardian shrine.” As a result, the then chief priest, Takahashi Jibu Shōsuke, was dismissed from his position as “Shimenegashira,” the coordinating head of the district’s priests, and Adatara Shrine was stripped of its title as the chief guardian shrine of Adachi District. “Illustrated History of Motomiya,” Chapter 3, Section 17 === Festival Dates === * May 1 * Three days from the fourth Friday of October At the Spring Festival on May 1, parishioners of Ukishima Shrine in the Shirazawa area of Motomiya City dedicate “Daidai Kagura.” During the Autumn Festival in October, three {{ill|lt=taikodai|Q11444734|qid=Q11444734|1=Taikodai}} floats traditionally handed down in areas of the former {{ill|lt=Nihonmatsu Domain|Q909007|qid=Q909007|lt=Nihonmatsu Domain territory|1=Nihonmatsu Domain}} appear and parade through the town ahead of the mikoshi procession. === Access === * Nearest station: {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Tōhoku Main Line|Q681190|qid=Q681190|1=Tōhoku Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Motomiya Station (Fukushima Prefecture)|Q873962|qid=Q873962|lt=Motomiya Station|1=Motomiya Station}} (15 minutes on foot) === Notes === {{Footnote help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.fukushima-jinjacho.or.jp/guidance/detail/04adatara.html Adatara Shrine] (Fukushima Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines) {{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Adatara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Adatara Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:本宮市の歴史]] [[Category:福島県の旧県社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:本宮市の建築物]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]]
